message:
feat(ft): tx execution engine with PaneStepExecutor integration and dry-run support

Replace the inline synthetic-only tx execution in both robot and
interactive tx run/rollback paths with a trait-based StepExecutor
architecture that can dispatch steps through real panes.

Major changes:
- Extract execute_tx_run_with_executor and execute_tx_rollback_with_executor
  helper functions that accept any StepExecutor implementor, enabling
  both synthetic fallback and real PaneStepExecutor dispatch
- Add --dry-run flag to robot tx run and tx run commands: stops after
  the prepare phase without executing commit/compensation, useful for
  validating plans before execution
- Wire PaneStepExecutor as the primary executor when a WezTerm mux
  connection and storage backend are available, with automatic fallback
  to synthetic execution when they are not
- Add structured tracing spans to tx run and rollback paths with
  executor type, timing, and outcome metadata for observability
- Add subprocess-bridge feature flag to Cargo.toml default features
- Add 4 new unit tests covering dry-run, full execution, fail-step
  with compensation, and rollback through RecordingStepExecutor mock

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -53,6 +53,7 @@ default = [
     "native-wezterm",
     "rollout",
     "semantic-search",

     "sync",
     "tui",
     "tui-dashboard",
@@ -74,6 +75,7 @@ rollout = ["frankenterm-core/rollout", "tui", "ftui"]
 metrics = ["frankenterm-core/metrics"]
 native-wezterm = ["frankenterm-core/native-wezterm"]
 distributed = ["frankenterm-core/distributed"]
+subprocess-bridge = ["frankenterm-core/subprocess-bridge"]
 sync = ["frankenterm-core/sync"]
 asupersync-runtime = ["frankenterm-core/asupersync-runtime"]
 semantic-search = ["frankenterm-core/semantic-search"]
